briefdescription.para: Determines if the specified version is valid.

detaileddescription.para:
StringPtr
points to the string to be validated.
TRUE if the version string is a valid format, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Unpacks a packed version into the specified version.

detaileddescription.para: Versions are packed into the configuration ROM to reduce the amount storage. A packed version is a binary format as opposed to a non-packed version which is implemented as a string.

parameterdescription.para: points to the version to unpack the packed version into.
contains the packed version to unpack.

briefdescription.para: Packs a version into the specified packed version.

detaileddescription.para: Versions are packed into the configuration ROM to reduce the amount storage.

InstancePtr
points to the version to pack.
PackedVersionPtr
points to the packed version which will receive the new packed version.
A status, XST_SUCCESS, indicating the packing was accomplished successfully, or an error, XST_INVALID_VERSION, indicating the specified input version was not valid such that the pack did not occur
The packed version pointed to by PackedVersionPtr is modified with the new packed version if the status indicates success.

briefdescription.para: Determines if two versions are equal.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the first version to be compared.
VersionPtr
points to a second version to be compared.
TRUE if the versions are equal, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Converts a version to a null terminated string.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the version to convert.
StringPtr
points to the string which will be the result of the conversion. This does not need to point to a null terminated string as an input, but must point to storage which is an adequate amount to hold the result string.
The null terminated string is inserted at the location pointed to by StringPtr if the status indicates success.
It is necessary for the caller to have already allocated the storage to contain the string. The amount of memory necessary for the string is specified in the version header file.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a version from a null terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: Since the string may not be a format which is compatible with the version, an error could occur.

InstancePtr
points to the version which is to be initialized.
StringPtr
points to a null terminated string which will be converted to a version. The format of the string must match the version string format which is X.YYX where X = 0 - 9, YY = 00 - 99, Z = a - z.
A status, XST_SUCCESS, indicating the conversion was accomplished successfully, or XST_INVALID_VERSION indicating the version string format was not valid.

briefdescription.para: Copies the contents of a version to another version.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
points to the version which is the source of data for the copy operation.
VersionPtr
points to another version which is the destination of the copy operation.
None.
